stem:
The table below shows three values of x and their corresponding values of y:

x: 1, 2, 3
y: 10, 13, 16

There is a linear relationship between x and y. Which equation represents this relationship?
rationale: Choice C is correct. The y-values increase by 3 each time x increases by 1, so the slope m = 3. Using point (1, 10) in y = mx + b gives 10 = 3(1) + b, so b = 7. The equation is y = 3x + 7. Choice A uses the y-value at x = 1 as the intercept. Choice B confuses the slope with one of the y-values. Choice D swaps slope and intercept.
